column4: Torrent Perat and Jiménez 2018 Section 3.2 Equation 4 reported M-19 hysteresis-loss fit against Cogent Electrical Steel manufacturer data under sinusoidal excitation
column5: The source identifies AISI M-19 as corresponding to EN 10106 M290-50A, and Reference 13 identifies Cogent Electrical Steel as the manufacturer-data source. It reports k_h 4.8, frequency exponent a 1.2, induction exponent n 2, and the printed output factor 10^-2 for Equation 4. The manufacturer loss data span 50 to 1000 Hz and 0.1 to 1.5 T. The paper does not identify a Cogent product designation or revision, production site, heat, chemistry, processing, anneal, coating, rolling direction, specimen, magnetic material test method, test temperature, repeats, dispersion, fit covariance, or coefficient compound units. It notes larger analytical-versus-experimental differences at high flux density. These numeric fields are source-equation inputs, not a portable executable model, generic M-19 authority, or B-H curve
